segment-text: EXT. JUNGLE - DAY

A machete slashes INTO FRAME. An American in battered fedora and leather jacket, accompanied by two gunbearers, hacks his way through dense bush. We see him from the back only. He hacks an opening, bats fly out AT CAMERA and the bushes part, revealing huge overgrown stone letters -the Mayan ruin look -- that spell "AIRPLANE II."
EXT. GANTRY - NIGHT
The Jupiter shuttle stands ready to fly.
HOUSTON, 2002 INT. MISSION CONTROL ROOM - STOCK FOOTAGE
of Houston Control with appropriate jargon V.O. INT. TERMINAL - WIDE ANGLE STOCK SHOT of a crowded modern terminal. All lunar departures, please proceed to
concourse lounge 'B.' EXT. TERMINAL - STOCK FOOTAGE - NIGHT

of heavy traffic at LAX. ANGLE ON TERMINAL DOORS A number of men in futuristic-looking mining outfits, carrying futuristic gear, unload a truck with a corporate logo that reads: TRX DEEP SPACE RESOURCE MANAGEMENT. Attention, all Pulsar Four mining
The miners look up.
Please report to the Resource Expeditions
office, level seven.
Two miners head to terminal doors and pass FOUR NUNS,
bid farewell to FATHER O'FLANAGAN. O'Flanagan shakes
hands of the first three elderly nuns, then grabs the young gorgeous nun and kisses her passionately. OLDER NUN
INT. CONTROL ROOM
A group tour moves through the room behind controllers.
All lunar shuttle landings are handled by these computers and simulated on these video units.
(at computer screen) You're programmed on R-two-niner and locked, Lunar eight six. Over.
INT. LUNAR SHUTTLE COCKPIT - NIGHT
Three crew members watch the lit landing strip as their shuttle approaches.
(to co-pilot) It's out of our hands now, gentlemen.
They smile.
INT. MISSION CONTROL
The CONTROLLER moves away from his video unit. A kid the tour who has lagged behind hits a switch. The screen turns into a video game with SFX. He flips knobs.
INT. LUNAR SHUTTLE
The crew are tossed from side to side and try to regain control of the ship.
EXT. NIGHT SKY
The Lunar shuttle careens towards the terminal, out of control.
INT. CONTROL ROOM

The kid is still playing like mad. A flash appears on the screen. SFX VIDEO GAME EXPLOSION and a corresponding EXPLOSION from outside. SIRENS WAIL. CONTROLLERS run around. The kid is oblivious and walks away.
EXT. TERMINAL - NIGHT
BUD KRUGER, head of the space center, and the
get out of a limo under a sign that reads, "MERCURY SHUTTLE." They walk and talk.
Commissioner, we both know the Mercury shuttle needs another month of pre-launch testing.
Forget it. The boys on the board want that shuttle to go on schedule.
INT. TERMINAL
Kruger and the Commissioner go up escalator.
And what do the boys on the board know about safety, Commissioner? Let me talk to them.
Bud, get wise to the political realities.
The boys on the board are under a lot of pressure from the boys downtown.
INT. TERMINAL - SECOND LEVEL
Kruger gets a pack of cigarettes from a machine. Commissioner buys a newspaper.
And I'll be the one they'll hang if there's a screw-up.
INT. TERMINAL - SHOESHINE STAND

Kruger and Commissioner get shoes shined. A MAN next them in white shoes reads paper with headline -- "SOLAR PLANT MELTDOWN, 500 WORKERS SERIOUSLY TANNED" -- and doesn't notice his shoes are getting black polish.
Listen, Bud, the boys downtown are under heavy fire from the boys in Washington.
That's why they're putting pressure on the boys on the board.
INT. TERMINAL
Kruger and Commissioner head down escalator.
Well, you tell the boys on the board to tell the boys downtown to let the boys in
Washington know that the press has been nosing around my people in the front office.
EXT. TERMINAL
Kruger and Commissioner head to their limo.
You handle your front office people, I'll handle the press and leave the boys in
Washington to the boys downtown and the
boys downtown to the boys on the board.
Commissioner.
They stop and look at each other.
What?
I just wish it was that simple.
DRAMATIC STING They get back in their limo and drive off.
INT. MISSION CONTROL
(over P.A.) This is Mercury One control. We have condition green. Mark launch 'T' minus two hours and counting. I repeat, we have condition green.
ANGLE ON LESLIE NEILSON - DOCTOR RUMACK
Dressed as a doctor and looking in a Controller's mouth. The Controller's face is bright green.
This condition isn't as bad as it could be if it were a lot worse. Take these pills. (hands him pills and water) Here's some water.
What is it, doctor?
Two parts oxygen, one part hydrogen. It'll make the pills go down easier.
EXT. GANTRY - CLOSEUP OF SHUTTLE - NIGHT
MUSIC:
BIG SPACE THEME
WIDE ANGLE LENS PANS the under-belly past the nose as if the viewer bent his head back as far as he could, until... CAMERA crashes to the ground as if it has fallen off tripod.

ANGLE ON SIDE OF SHIP MUSIC: BIG SPACE THEME STARTS FROM TOP AGAIN PAN workers on scaffolding who check gauges on exterior of ship. PAN to panel that reads, "SOLID FUEL CHUTE" -- a sweaty muscular worker in undershirt opens the panel, flames shoot out. LOOSEN to reveal another sweaty worker in undershirt shoveling coal into the chute. PAN to other workers checking more gauges. SUDDENLY a PANEL EXPLODES. Smoking, sparking wires pop out. A WORKER runs up with walkie-talkie in hand.
(into walkie-talkie) This is Mercury station six! Sarg and fast!
EXT. TERMINAL - NIGHT

SIMON KURTZ and ELAINE THOMPSON get out of a taxi with hand luggage. They are dressed in matching flight outfits. A JAPANESE COUPLE get out of a taxi -- the man has about fifty cameras around his neck. His wife hangs another camera on him. He crashes to the ground under the weight. Simon and Elaine are approached by a BUSINESSMAN with handful of roses.
Would you like to buy a rose to help bail out Chrysler? We're an all-profit organization and need all the money we can get.
Simon hands the Businessman a dollar and takes a rose.
(to Businessman) Here.
The Businessman joins six other BUSINESSMEN carrying signs with LEE IACCOCA's picture. They all smile and chant.
(to Hari Krishna) IACCOCA, IACCOCA, IACCOCA, IARAMA.
Simon hands Elaine the rose. She takes his arm as they
towards the sign that reads, "MERCURY SHUTTLE."
For the best little computer officer on the Mercury mission.
(smiling) Simon.
Who would believe that Elaine Thompson was once a stewardess on the Denver-Chicago run.
And I can hardly believe that I'm engaged to someone like you, Simon. I'm a very lucky woman.
A man with a suitcase on a leash walks beside them. case pulls him along like a dog and takes a leak on a post.
Women and the space program have come a long way, sweetheart. But after the wedding, no more complicated computers for my little girl.
But, darling, they've offered me a chance to head up the computer analysis division for the Jupiter probe.
You're heading up the division in charge of babies for Mr. and Mrs. Simon Kurtz.
He kisses Elaine and smiles.
And that's an order, Lieutenant.
The man with the case on a leash passes a woman with a
on a leash. The cases start a violent, BARKING DOG FIGHT.
EXT. REAR OF TERMINAL BUILDING - NIGHT
SARG (imagine George Kennedy), dressed in a tuxedo, tie undone, with a huge cigar, which he eats during the conversation, talks to his wife on his car phone. A
in hard hat waits next to him. Behind the worker are drums and a large sign that reads: DANGER, FUEL -ABSOLUTELY NO SMOKING, ABSOLUTELY NO SPITTING.
SPLIT SCREEN WITH WIFE AT HOME.
(in evening gown, a cigar stuck in her mouth) We've got a condition red with our marriage. I think you're in love with that shuttle, not with me. I want a divorce, Sarg.
Sarg lights his cigar.
Is that it?
Sarg throws the lit match towards the "ABSOLUTELY NO SMOKING" sign. The worker grimaces but nothing happens.
Sarg, there's been someone else for fifteen years. You were just too involved in your work to see it.

They hang up. Sarg bites off the end of his cigar and spits it in the direction of the "NO SPITTING" sign, blowing worker OUT OF FRAME with a MASSIVE EXPLOSION. Sarg hops into his car, takes a Lava lamp from the seat and puts the car roof -- like Kojak's flasher. The lamp flashes, the SIREN WAILS, as Sarg pulls away.
INT. SARG'S CAR - ON THE MOVE - NIGHT

He thinks about his wife. SPLIT SCREEN FLASHBACK OF THEIR BEDROOM. Marge, a cigar in her mouth, is in bed with football players -- other half-clad athletes (hockey, baseball, wrestling, basketball) line up for their turn her. Sarg has to climb over them to kiss her goodbye.
You just can't see it, can you, Sarg. We'll talk tomorrow, hon.
EXT. TERMINAL, PASSENGER ARRIVAL AREA - NIGHT
The WILSON FAMILY -- JOHN, ALICE and ten-year-old JIMMY -carry luggage. Jimmy carries a puppy in a small cage.
All Mercury passengers please proceed to
passenger processing, concourse level 'C.'
That's us.
Jimmy Wilson looks at Scraps.
Will Scraps be able to sit with us, Dad?
We'll have to check, Jimmy. long trip to Mercury.
A PORTER approaches.
Can I help you folks?
(handing him a bag)
It's a pretty
(noticing Scraps) Is that your puppy, son?
Yeah, his name is Scraps and he's going to Mercury with us.
No dogs are allowed on the shuttle, son.
But they said...
(pulling out a handgun) Scraps will have to be shot. for you here if you like.
He SHOOTS.
Scraps!!!
(laughing) Just joking. Blanks. fine.
See, Scraps is Alice, John, and Porter crack up while Jimmy cries.
It was just a joke, Jimmy.
Alice and John look at each other as if there is something
A man lifts a large dog cage out of the trunk of a
Inside is another man dressed in S&M leathers.
(to Man in Cage) If we can't fit you under the seat, you'll just have to be locked up with the luggage. The man in the cage seems to relish the latter suggestion.
Two Porters lift the cage onto a conveyor belt next to Jimmy's dog.
EXT. GANTRY - NIGHT
Sarg stands beside the burnt panel on the side of the shuttle. He is eating another cigar, holding a burnt
and talking into a walkie-talkie. There's no way this wiring could have passed inspection without Simon Kurtz's okay!
SPLIT SCREEN OF KRUGER IN HIS OFFICE

(on phone) Just patch up the damage and get that ship ready to fly. That's an order, mister! You've got it, mister. But you can tell your boys on the board for me that this thing stinks to high heaven of kickback.
KRUGER'S OFFICE - FULL SCREEN

Kruger turns to room. LOOSEN to reveal a dozen fiveyearold boys in three-piece suits, smoking cigars. A huge Keans-style painting of three astronauts with big eyes, painted on black velvet, hangs in b.g.
You heard it, boys. Now, what do you say? Fuck him!
EXT. GANTRY - NIGHT
Sarg looks at the wire and shakes his head.
Sarg spits a chunk of cigar on the ground, blowing the worker OUT OF FRAME with a MASSIVE EXPLOSION.
EXT. WILD BLUE YONDER - DAY
A jet fighter streaks across the sky.
INT. JET - DAY

TED STRIKER is at the controls. His oxygen mask hangs loose from his helmet. Clouds outside shoot by -- he's going least 700 MPH. A bird lands on the nose in front of window. Ted shoos it away by knocking on the glass. He takes a slug of beer and cockily pulls on the stick.
EXT. FIGHTER - DAY
The plane does a barrel roll.
INT. FIGHTER - DAY
Ted has beer all over his face and shirt.
EXT. SMALL LATIN-AMERICAN LANDING STRIP - DAY "SOUTH AMERICA, 2002 (PALUKAVILLE)"
Striker's fighter comes in for a landing.
EXT. OUTSKIRTS OF RIO MONTENEGRO - DAY
CLOSEUP of sign, "WELCOME TO RIO MONTENEGRO -- POP. 2,354,900 -- NO TORTURING 7AM-9AM MON.-FRI."
INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY SUPER: "HOTEL MONTENEGRO"
COME UP on CLOSEUP of parachute. PAN past a flight
thrown over a chair, clothes strewn around, a framed of TED and ELAINE on the bedtable next to a bottle of whiskey. The room is hot and dusty. Ted lies on the
soaked with sweat, a drink in his hand, a cigarette
from his lip. He takes a drink and, forgetting to the cigarette, swallows it without batting an eye.
to find a CRUCIFIX above the bed with a real person on it.
(to self) Who could have figured it would come to this. It'll be twenty years this week that I lost my entire squadron over Macho Grande. Planes, too.
DOG FIGHT FOOTAGE
You're too low, Striker! You're too low!
The Crucifix in b.g. looks around trying to figure out
the planes in the SUPER are coming from.
After the war, I couldn't go near anything with a pair of wings. HIM.
TED IN PARK TRYING TO AVOID PIGEONS THAT FOLLOW A pigeon lands on the Cross.
That is, until fate dropped me on the seat of my pants at the stick of a 167 into Chicago with no crew.
TED LANDING PLANE IN "AIRPLANE!"
It's funny how fate can make heroes out of cowards.
The Crucifix gives a look of exasperated boredom. SUPER: NEW YORK TIMES HEADLINE, "STRIKER SAVES l20!" OVER A PHOTO OF TED AND ELAINE WAVING.
Then came the job offers.
SUPER: HOUSTON TRIBUNE HEADLINE, "FLYING HEROES ACCEPT NASA POSTS" OVER A PHOTO OF TED AND ELAINE WAVING.
The publicity.
SUPER: NATIONAL ENQUIRER HEADLINE, "TED IMPOTENT?
FRIGID?" A MINOR HEADLINE READS, "JFK ALIVE, SAYS UROLOGIST!"
Even the key to the City of New York.
MAYOR HANDING TED A KILO OF GRASS.
Now look at me.
Ted wipes the sweat off his face and switches on the table fan. A tornado force wind destroys the room as he fights to switch off the fan.
EXT. TOWN SQUARE - DAY "RIO MONTENEGRO - TOWN SQUARE"

A peasant boy takes a newspaper from a newsstand. headline reads, "MERCURY SHUTTLE FLIES TOMORROW." BOY Senor Ted! The boy hightails it through the town-square and -- A large alabaster statue of the Generalissimo snorting
He passes a line of peasants held at gunpoint by soldiers. We HOLD on them. In b.g. are stores like HITLER'S SHOES, TRIEU GUYS FROM SAIGON LIQUORS, KEY'S KEYS -- GENERAL stands in doorway.
SUBTITLES -- "Drug dealer or communist?"
(pleading) Traficante de drugas, traficante de drugas!
SUBTITLES -- "Drug dealer, drug dealer!" The soldier lets the peasant go.
SUBTITLES -- "Drug dealer or communist?" The peasant panics and bolts from the line. FIRE.
Communista!
He runs up to THE HOTEL MONTENEGRO. A sign reads, "TV, POOL, WATERBEDS, DONKEY, KLEENEX" -- All but "KLEENEX" are crossed out. He runs inside.
INT. HOTEL LOBBY
A clean-cut AMERICAN COUPLE argue with Desk Clerk.
(to Desk Clerk) We've lost all our travelers' checks! What are we going to do?! Calm down.
What kind were they?
American Excess!
The Clerk throws up his hands and strikes the same pose the Karl Marden look-alike in the "AMERICAN EXCESS" poster,
I'd say you're fucked.
The boy passes them and bounds up the stairs.
INT. HOTEL ROOM
CLOSEUP of Ted pacing.

(to self) Next thing I know, I'm the chief test pilot for the XR-2300, NASA's first Mercury shuttle. That is, until I report she's got more flaws than the Titanic. LOOSEN slowly to find the Cross empty and the man who was on
it hanging, his feet dangling next to Ted.
Now I'm testing old F-l8s for some crazy Generalissimo who thinks there's a commie behind every tree in the Amazon.
SUDDENLY the boy bursts in. Senor Ted!
Look!
Ted spins and the paper stays still. He stops.
My God! They're launching the ZR-2300. Do you know what that is, Jose? BOY The muffler bracket for a '78 Pinto.
No, that's an XR-2200. The XR-2300 is the Mercury shuttle. They can't do it!
EXT. HOTEL MONTENEGRO
Ted races out.
Taxi!
SFX: A DOZEN CARS SCREECH AND CRASH INTO EACH OTHER. HORNS STICK.
ANGLE ON PILE OF CARS
Ted jumps into the taxi on bottom of pile. The driver POUNDS on his HORN -- it CONTINUES TO BLARE as they pull away, dragging other taxis.
Houston, Texas.
Houston, Texas, senor??
Right at the corner, left at the light. Here's a map and step on it!
Ted throws a map onto the floor of the front seat. driver steps on the map.
The EXT. TERMINAL, PASSENGER ARRIVALS - NIGHT
An old truck loaded high with furniture pulls up. A YOUNG MAN and his PARENTS, looking like the family from THE
OF WRATH, get out.
We finally made it, Ma. Mercury. We're gonna start all over agin. A whole new life.
Where the soil is rich.
He bends down and takes a handful of earth from the terminal sidewalk and lets it run through his fingers.
And the union strong. Where men are free
to realize their true potential. A black PORTER approaches and grabs a bag at the bottom of their pile of junk.
Can I help you?
Where black men and white men, working together, can move mountains.

The Porter pulls the bag and the entire mountain of junk cascades to the ground. ANGLE ON TED'S TAXI ARRIVING It is covered in mud, flames shoot out from under the hood, the HORN BLARES. The driver flips off the meter which reads, "874,567,004,500,000,000." The numbers run off meter along a special attachment.
That'll be eight hundred and seventy-four zillion quastavitas.
Here's three bucks.
Keep the change.
Thank you!!
INT. TERMINAL
STELLA BARRINGTON pushes her wheelchair-ridden father, DR. CYRUS BARRINGTON, through the terminal crowd. A PORTER approaches.
You folks need any help?
Thanks, but we have a terrific woman in on Thursdays.
Say, isn't that Dr. Barrington, the worldrenowned agronomist?
Yes.
It's a privilege to meet you, sir, I'm familiar with all your work.
Let's go, Daddy. We have to check in. (to Porter) He was never appreciated at the Institute.
Ah, yes, the Institute, I'm familiar with it.
Now he's D-Y-I-N-Ging and wants to be buried on Mercury.
Stella pushes her father away as another PORTER approaches our first Porter.
Say, wasn't that Dr. Barrington, the world-renowned agronomist? That's right. work?
Are you familiar with his
No, but the missus sure is.
ANGLE ON TED AT ARRIVALS/DEPARTURES MONITORS The departures side reads. "MERCURY SHUTTLE - DEPARTS
(to self) I have to stop that flight.
Ted pushes his way through the crowd past two businessmen. HOLD on them as BUSINESSMAN 1 straightens BUSINESSMAN tie and hair.
Don't worry about the Viatex account. It's a buy-sell option. We can't get hurt in either case.
Just keep on top of their legal people, Bob.
Don't sweat it.
They kiss and part. ANGLE ON "1ST CLASS" TICKET LINE
(to traveller) We can take your in-flight dinner orders here, if you like. Today there's lobster or rack of lamb.
The lobster sounds nice. The Agent throws a huge lobster into a steaming
(rushing up) Where can I find mission control?
(without pointing or indicating in any way) Information's over there.
He seems a bit puzzled but moves on. ANGLE ON "2ND CLASS" LINE Ted passes travellers in rags.
(to traveller in rags) Today there's the swill or the slop.
The slop sounds nice.
Ted passes the "NO CLASS" line. Travellers in line all
gaudy double-knit suits, white belts and shoes, carry
lamps and bongo drums, wear alpine hats, etc. The
hands a NO CLASS traveller his ticket with large foam
dangling. Ted spots the INFORMATION counter and heads
it.
(to Info Agent) What's the fastest animal on earth? The cheetah.
Next.
Should I fake my orgasms? Yes.
Next in line are three armed, bearded "TERRORISTS." "TERRORIST" 1
The TERRORISTS leave looking confused.
Mission control?
(without indicating anything) It's over therrrrrrr!
The agent is shot with an arrow and slumps over the CLOSEUP OF TED He looks in the CAMERA. MUSIC: DRAMATIC STING.
That's strange. I just came from over there.
INT. TERMINAL - ANOTHER AREA
Elaine, Simon, and FRANK MORGAN, the shuttle pilot, talk.
I'll meet you on board. business to attend to.
I've got some Frank leaves.
(to Elaine) Frank's the best pilot in the program.
I'm so excited, Simon.
I guess this is a first for you.
No, I've been excited before.
Elaine.
(turning around) Te...!
That's not important now, Elaine. to talk.
Simon pulls Elaine away.
They all walk and talk.
Ted, we've been worried sick ever since you escaped from the Ronald Reagan Institute For The Mentally Feeble.
They walk faster. The b.g. starts whipping by as if
running. Wind blows in their hair.
Are you on the Mercury mission?
That's right, Striker. And we're getting married when we return.
The b.g. moves faster. The wind gets stronger. They pass a marathon refreshment stand and are handed wet sponges and cups of Gatoraid.
It's got to be stopped!
But, Ted, the invitations have already gone out.
I mean the Mercury flight. and, Kurtz, you know why.
It's not safe
You're still crazy, Striker. sweetheart. Come on,
Simon pulls Elaine away as she looks back with empathy.
What did Ted mean? They leave Ted standing.
Elaine, he's still sick.
The wind is still blowing in his hair even though he stands still. Ted has a FLASHBACK.
INT. ROCKET HANGAR
Ted, in flight coveralls, follows after Simon who is overseeing the operation.
You're seeing bugs where they don't exist, Striker.
(holding wire) Look at this wiring. It's shorting out under high temperatures.
You're tired, Striker, overworked. wiring meets all the safety specifications.
I know you've been subtly spreading the word that I'm having a breakdown.
A NEWSPAPER BOY passes.
Shuttle test pilot goes mad!
A TECHNICIAN -- JACOBS -- enters with drawings of flight outfits.
Sir, these are the designs for the flight uniforms. I think the captain's is to die.
(with drawings) Good, good, no, no, good. What's this?! I said no studded dog collars, Jacobs.
Jacobs grabs the drawings and leaves in a huff.
You won't get away with this, Simon!
Ted turns to leave and bumps right into Elaine. holds him.
Ted, what's wrong?
(pulling away from her) Ask Simon.
In b.g. a car is parked by a huge rocket engine. A worker opens the hood and strings jumper cables to the rocket. sign above rocket reads, "ROCKET ENGINE TEST AREA."
Ted, you're overworked. You've been flying yourself into the ground.
There's nothing wrong with me!
Let's relax tonight, just the two of us. I'll make a quiet Italian dinner just the way you like it, with spaghetti.
You're as bad as the rest of them, Elaine! (ranting) It's all here in the design (grabs plans from a passing technician) Look! It's all here!
Simon signals the guards. They grab Ted. He struggles rants. A DOCTOR in white approaches and injects
into Ted's arm.
You're hurting him!
Ted falls unconscious. Simon holds Elaine.
Elaine! Ted's a danger to himself, he's a threat to this mission and his behavior does absolutely nothing to promote peace in the Middle East.
(crying on Simon's shoulder) Simon, why has he become so... so...
So mentally ill?

Elaine collapses on Simon's shoulder, sobbing. Simon smiles evilly. The technician with the jumper cables, b.g., signals the driver to start the car. The car revs and the rocket fires. DISSOLVE BACK TO:
Ted still standing alone and muttering.
(to self) I'm perfectly sane.
Ted notices Stella Barrington looking at him.
Excuse me, are you alright? I noticed you talking to yourself. I'm a nurse. Can I be of some help?
Uh... oh, thank you.
It's nothing.
You don't have to thank me, I'm a nurse.
This is my father, Dr. Barrington.
Not Dr. Barrington, the world renowned agronomist?
Yes. He's dying a-n-d wants to be buried on Mercury.
I'm familiar with your work, Doctor. You'll have to excuse me, I have to go.
You don't have to excuse yourself. nurse. I understand.
Ted leaves.
INT. TERMINAL - OUTSIDE DRUGSTORE
Meet me onboard, sweetheart. I have to pick up a few things at the drugstore.
(checking her watch) Don't be too long.
Simon walks into the drugstore past a DRUGGIST who whispers
Uppers, downers, coke, speed, hash, Tampax.
Ted approaches. booth. We see light flashes from a 25C/ photo
Elaine.
Ted, please. You're just making things difficult for yourself.
A WOMAN IN BLACK GARTER BELT emerges from photo booth followed by a donkey.
Elaine, what happened to us?

Ted, I loved you and I'll always love you. But I need Simon. He's stable. He's a good provider. I want that at this stage of the game, Ted. He might have his faults, but Simon doesn't know the meaning of the word fear and I need that in a man.
INT. DRUGSTORE
The Druggist hands Simon a huge bottle of pills.
reads, "ANTI-FEAR PILLS."
(looking at the label, "ANTIFEAR PILLS") What does this word mean?
(looking at label) 'Fear,' to be afraid or over-anxious.
Simon swallows a handful of pills, braces himself and
INT. TERMINAL
Ted is still following Elaine.
Elaine, someone has to listen to me. going right to mission control.
A kid bops by with a huge (5'x3'x2') oak cabinet, TV/stero console perched on his shoulder. MUSIC:
Ted, you should go right back to the hospital.
Elaine leaves and Ted has another FLASHBACK.
INT. MENTAL HOSPITAL

PAN by dorm window. We see a sign on grounds outside "THE RONALD REAGAN INSTITUTE OF SUPPLY-SIDE ECONOMICS AND HOSPITAL FOR THE CRIMINALLY INSANE." PAN patient in bed, accountant at desk, patient in bed, accountant at desk,
in bed. Ted has electrodes attached to his head. A
with "I (heart) NORMAL" on the back of her uniform, hands
Ted some pills. He is about to pop them when she stops
That's $38.
Ted hands her bills. LOOSEN to find Elaine on other side of bed. She turns off the radio and opens a box of spaghetti-to-go.
Eat this spaghetti, Ted. feel a lot better.
It'll make you GROANING O.S.
Who's that, Ted? TED Sammy Davis Junior. Terrible car accident. He hasn't been the same since.
ANGLE ON SAMMY DAVIS A white patient in lots of gold chains. A Nurse stands him and pulls a long oil dip-stick from his mouth,
it, wipes it off and replaces it. RETURN TO SCENE
Elaine, when are you going to realize Simon Kurtz put me in here to get me out of the way.
And when are you going to realize, Ted, that your mental hygiene is the most important thing right now. VOICE (O.S.) (ranting) It works... No, it doesn't... Yes, it does.
What's his problem?
On BEARDED MAN in rags chained to wall. MAN It does work... No, it doesn't.
On Ted and Elaine.
His name's David Stockman. He's been here twenty years, that's all he says.

Ted, you must remember what the doctor said, the first step on the road to sanity is admitting that you're sick. Now take your electro-shock and you'll be back at the space center in no time. And by the way, Ted, I'm leaving you for Simon.
Ted gags on his spaghetti.
I just can't go on living with a man who refuses to deal with reality, Ted. I have to go now. Believe me, it's best for all concerned.
Elaine leans over to kiss Ted.
No goodbyes, Elaine.
He turns away. Just go.
If that's the way you want it.
That's the way I want it.
Just turn the radio on and go. Goodbye, Ted.
I don't want to hurt you.
Elaine flips a switch by the bed thinking it's the radio. It's the "ELECTRO-SHOCK." Ted goes into convulsions as she leaves.
TED AT TERMINAL
He rubs his temples.
INT. TERMINAL - OUTSIDE BOOKSTORE

Frank Morgan kisses JANE DENNIS, his mistress. In b.g. is a bookstore window with a display of yellow covered books plain black titles and a sign advertising, "NO-NAME BOOKS." Visible titles include: "DEEP BOOK," "FASCINATING BOOK," "THE WORLD ACCORDING TO JOHN DOE." A man next to the window reads a newspaper with headline, "SENSELESS MURDER UP
AND RISING!"
